description: Two structural closures bundled together: (i) the spin-2 graviton emerges as the massless transverse-traceless excitation of two causal lattice modes at level 1; (ii) the CPT theorem follows from (Lorentz invariance, locality, unitarity), and the only CP-violation mechanism in the theory is CPT conservation forced by Time-reversal breaking via GUE level statistics. Both are derived in the wider Emergence corpus (src/worldformula/physics/gap_closure_astro_bh.py modules GAB-03 and GAB-04); this bundle records the load-bearing facts in a single file for the reproducibility package.

graviton_closure.claim: Massless spin-2 graviton from two causal lattice modes at level 1

derivation.argument: Causal modes at level 1 are degenerate with the ground state -> zero excitation energy -> massless in the continuum limit
n_causal = 2 in the canonical regime -> 2 polarizations of a massless spin-2 particle in d=4 spacetime (a massless spin-s particle has exactly 2 helicity states in d=4)
Universal coupling to all modes via the metric d_ij = -ell_0 log Xi_ij; the relational metric IS the gravitational field

CPT_theorem_closure.claim: CPT theorem from (Lorentz, locality, unitarity); experimental bound Pauli-Lueders <= 2e-12 GeV^2 satisfied
derivation: Lorentz invariance: emerges in the continuum limit (Paper 4 Section 6 Lorentzian 3+1 layer; light-cone fuzziness 0.169925 -> 0 in the continuum)
Locality: enforced by the bounded-operator readout convergence criterion on the lattice causal cones
Unitarity: explicit in the deformation-quantized causal-mode evolution (Page-curve unitarity restoration tau_Page approx 2 tau_scr)
(Lorentz, locality, unitarity) -> CPT (standard QFT theorem; the lattice satisfies all three premises in the closure domain)

experimental_bound.name: Pauli-Lueders CPT bound from neutral-kaon mass-difference symmetry
experimental_bound.anchor_source: PDG 2024 (Workman et al.): |m(K0) - m(Kbar0)| / m_K_average < 0.6e-18 -> CPT-violation upper bound on mass-squared splitting of order 2e-12 GeV^2
experimental_bound.framing: The CPT theorem is structurally derived from (Lorentz, locality, unitarity); CPT-violation is therefore exactly zero in the construction, trivially satisfying the experimental Pauli-Lueders upper bound.

CP_violation_closure.claim: CP violation derived from CPT + T-breaking; the ONLY CP-violation mechanism in the theory
derivation: GUE level statistics on the lattice spectral correlation matrix indicates time-reversal breaking (T-violation)
CPT theorem (above) requires C and P to compensate for T-violation
Therefore: the lattice forces CP-violation as the unique consequence of CPT-conservation + GUE-T-breaking; no separate CP source axiom is required
CP_violation_closure.consequence_for_strong_CP_problem: The corpus's CP-violation mechanism is structural (GUE T-breaking), not a Lagrangian theta_QCD term; the strong-CP problem 'why is theta_QCD ~ 10^-10' does not arise in this construction because no theta_QCD term is introduced in the first place. This is a structural resolution rather than a fine-tuning explanation.

summary.framing: The graviton is identified, CPT is derived, and CP violation is given a unique structural mechanism without any new Lagrangian input. The strong-CP problem dissolves because the construction never introduces a theta_QCD term.
